concepts.name: Conversation slot is keyed by the original message id (stable anchor across edits)
Revision ordering is driven solely by the rev clock (max-rev wins; delete-wins ties; higher replacement id breaks edit/edit ties; sender-clamped monotonic)

reviewer_findings.description: S3 OBLIGATION (from S2 review): (1) MARKER-FIRST kind-5 classification — the edit-marked companion carries BOTH a bare ['e',orig] and marked ['e',orig,'','edit']; S3 must classify on 'any edit-marked e-tag => discard companion; lone-unmarked kind-5 => delete', NEVER on the bare-tag presence, or every edit self-deletes its slot (inverse of AC-DEL-7). (2) CAP INCOMING REV AT INGEST — clampRev saturates at lastKnownRev>=2^53 (float precision); an author's hostile/broken device could poison a slot's stored rev to a huge finite value making all later edits equal-rev ties. S3 must cap an accepted signal's rev to wallSeconds + a max-skew constant before persisting.

concepts.name: Reconciliation outcome is independent of arrival order for {original, delete, edit-replacement}
Pending-signal buffer is bounded by cap + TTL, keyed per target id, collapsing to max-rev; delete persists a marker on expiry, edit materializes in place
applyDeleteEditSignal(thread, rumor) — single React-free/context-free reconciliation entrypoint consumed by both transports
Author-only authorization (AC-AUTH-2) evaluated at application time; pending signals held unauthenticated until target arrives

reviewer_findings.description: AC-AUTH-2 GROUP is best-effort only: marmot-ts 0.5.1 drops the MLS sender leaf for application messages so the inner rumor pubkey is group-member-attested, NOT identity-bound (verified vs ts-mls source). Product-owner adjudicated 2026-07-07: SHIP with documented trust (DMs secure via seal sig; MLS bars non-members; same posture as existing kind-9/kind-7). Tracked upstream: BACKLOG marmot-ts-0-5-1-drops. Not a blocker.

concepts.name: User delete is a field flip (tombstoned:true) retaining the row; removeMessages hard-delete is reserved for the ADR-001/002 purge path
A re-delivered original (no rev) must not clobber an edited/tombstoned slot (AC-STORE-3)
update-in-place / tombstone primitive on chatPersistence, enqueued on the per-thread appendQueues
Delete/edit rumor tag shapes: unmarked kind-5 = delete; edit-marked replacement + edit-marked companion kind-5; e-tags original + every prior replacement id; k tag = target kind
Edit replacement wire created_at (seconds) is pinned to the original and MUST NOT double as the rev clock
Dispatcher handlers under lib/marmot/handlers/ import nothing from context/; deps arrive via HandlerDeps injection
ChatBox is the single composer owner for DM and group; edit-mode composer seam defined once
UI copy stays neutral — never implies enforced/guaranteed deletion or non-Few honoring (AC-INTEROP-1/2)

findings.description: updateMessageInPlace is merge-not-replace: a patch omitting tombstoned/edited leaves those flags set. S3 MUST pass tombstoned:false explicitly when a higher-rev edit supersedes a lower-rev tombstone, or the winning edit stays hidden by filterVisibleMessages. Storage enforces no rev monotonicity — all rev ordering / delete-wins ties are S3's job. Carried into S3 brief + verification.
filterVisibleMessages docstring pins reactions/api.ts:165 (line-pinned cross-file ref, rot risk). Unactioned (below sev-5 noise floor).

S3 OBLIGATION (carried to S3 brief): storage provides an atomic strictly-older-rev FLOOR + real-signal clobber-guard; S3 owns equal-rev tie resolution (delete-wins; higher replacement-id). Flags are merge-only: S3 must send tombstoned:false EXPLICITLY to un-tombstone, and MUST NOT emit an un-tombstone patch at EQUAL rev (spec 2.5 requires strictly-exceeds; delete wins ties). filterVisibleMessages still not wired into render (deferred to S3/S6, VQ-S1-012).

companion kind-5 emits originalId bare+marked (diverges from spec 2.4 literal list; behaviorally inert, documented+tested). Deferred to S3 as MARKER-FIRST classifier obligation; curator may reconcile spec 2.4 wording.
ms-vs-seconds + fractional guards added (Number.isInteger + MAX_PLAUSIBLE_UNIX_SECONDS); assertRealRev integer-tightened; e-tag dedup added. All resolved in remediation round 1.

surfaces_touched: app/src/lib/messageEdits/api.ts::resolvePendingSignalsForSlot(deferred-auth hook; resolves slotId rumor-group before sweep)
app/tests/unit/messageEdits/api.test.ts(new, ~90 tests incl 24-perm order-independence + fault-injection crash tests)

findings.description: [3 remediation rounds, all RESOLVED] crash-window delete-loss class: (r1) permanent divergence on missed resolve, mid-sweep drop, stale-meta un-tombstone; (r2) marker self-heal gap + removePendingByRumorId crossing skipTargetId; (r3) remove-before-persist on shared resolver. All closed via self-healing marker-aware unification + persist-then-remove + storage-truth tie + rev-cap + hygiene caps/teardown. Comparator was correct throughout.

ACCEPTED RESIDUALS (both reviewers, below threshold): (1) cap-eviction known-row branch marker-blind but self-healing (tightened w/ read-only marker-rev check; transient over-visibility corrected next sweep, no delete-loss, no write race); (2) edit-vs-edit D15 equal-rev tie reads slot-meta — content-only divergence on exact same-second two-device edit collision landing on a crash window (would require extending S1 frozen ChatMessage contract to close). Plus poisoned-clock AC-ORDER-3 best-effort residual (documented in spec).

surfaces_touched: ContactChat.tsx inbound kind-5 + edit-marked-kind-14 (historical+live), resolve-after-append, awaited thread-open sweep, own-msg auth guards, __fewDmMessageEdits bridge

findings.description: [2 remediation rounds, RESOLVED] render-clobber on BOTH historical (reconcileHistoricalBatch) and live (resolveFreshOriginalFromStorage) paths — re-delivered original of a since-deleted/edited message re-rendered; stale-rev clamp starvation (resolveAuthoritativeRev); persist-fail masquerading as publish-fail (swallowed); shadow-init tests replaced with real pure-function tests; own-msg auth guards; forked marker predicate unified. All closed; both models converged clean.

S5 OBLIGATION (carried): the group inbound MLS-application append path MUST mirror the same resolve-after-append + storage-substitution discipline (re-delivered kind-9 original of a since-deleted/edited group message would otherwise re-render). Group render flows through ChatStoreContext (not ContactChat), so resolveFreshOriginalFromStorage is not directly reusable — same obligation, different substitution point. Also: AC-DEL-8 priorReplacementIds=[] is correct-by-design (product decision: non-Few best-effort); S5 must clone [] too, NOT track a chain.

ACCEPTED RESIDUAL: sub-ms crash window between successful publish and durable applyDeleteEditSignal (author-device-only; author re-action recovers; DM signal gift-wrapped to peer so no inbound self-heal). Flagged for S7 (don't assume durability across a crash in that window).

surfaces_touched: deleteEditHandler.ts (new RumorHandler<5>, zero context imports, unconditional route to S3, honest auth comment)
chatHandler.ts (edit-marked kind-9 dispatch-routing + resolve-after-append + unconditional version-bump)

ChatStoreContext.tsx (performGroupDeleteMessage/performGroupEditMessage exported pure fns, reconcileMessagesWithStorage at all 3 storage-set points, awaited thread-open sweep, sendMessage/sendImageMessage resolve-after-append, __fewGroupMessageEdits bridge)

findings.description: [1 remediation round, RESOLVED] group render/state gaps: thread-open sweep projection (S4-parity), optimistic-delete resurrection tail, shadow-init tests (extracted performGroup* pure fns), resolve-after-append convention drift on sendMessage/sendImageMessage, sweep-side-effects-on-other-slots (unconditional bump), post-edit tombstone drop. All closed; both models clean.

ACCEPTED (product-adjudicated): AC-AUTH-2 GROUP is best-effort/group-member-attested — marmot-ts drops MLS sender leaf; inner pubkey not identity-bound. SHIP decision 2026-07-07; documented in AC-AUTH-2/MV-3/handler-comment; tracked BACKLOG marmot-ts-0-5-1-drops. DMs fully secure.
ACCEPTED RESIDUAL: durable-write crash window (both transports) — crash between publish and durable apply leaves author-local state stale; recoverable by re-action. Flagged for S7.

surfaces_touched: ChatBox.tsx (action menu, edit composer, edited marker, two-click delete confirm, filterVisibleMessages backstop, allowMessageActions gate, threadId reset effect, delete-while-edit cancel, paste/drop edit-gate)
i18n.ts (12 keys en+de incl chatSend, listPreviewStructured)

findings.description: [1 remediation round, RESOLVED] cross-surface UI bugs the pure-fn tests couldn't see: FEEDBACK-CHANNEL BYPASS (edit/delete published unmarked signals into the sealed feedback channel — matches project memory feedback_channel_reused_chat_gating; closed via allowMessageActions gate); Edit-on-structured-announcements (raw-JSON corruption; closed via canEditMessage parseStructured reject); raw-JSON list preview; edit-state-survives-thread-switch (silent data loss); delete-while-editing; paste/drop mid-edit; +2 i18n/case nits. All 13 owned ACs verified by JSX inspection by both models; converged clean.

ACCEPTED RESIDUAL: list-preview refreshes on list-page mount, not live (the list feature didn't exist before; list pages carry no live subscription). AC-LIST-1's 'processed occurs on next open' relaxation covers this. VQ-S6-020.

[1 remediation round, RESOLVED] e2e hardening: ordering-proxy vacuous-pass closed via positive control message; sampled never-rendered replaced with gap-free MutationObserver latch (installed before nav); image-helper before-set guards; pre-edit content/marker assertions. 16/16 green on Docker harness (run 3x total across impl+remediation). Owned ACs AC-DEL-1/AC-EDIT-1 solidly covered; closes S6's VQ-S6-023 JSX-wiring gap via real-UI clicks. Both models converged clean.

ACCEPTED: 3s waitForTimeout in the AC-ORDER e2e proxy retained (no relay-readiness marker exists) but documented + tied to AC-60 precedent; AC-ORDER-1/3 owned+deterministically tested by S3. Two 5s beforeAll setup waits template-inherited from groups-reactions.spec (setup-only, fail-visible). CONVENTION: delete-readiness = disappearance of msg-<id> (no tombstone placeholder testid, AC-DEL-3 silent removal) — a future delete-placeholder would invalidate expectMessageGone.
